_meta.note: Built to the Armagh-locked standard. Wexford lost the Leinster quarter-final heavily to Louth, then ran to the Tailteann Cup quarter-final (beat Limerick, lost to Fermanagh, beat Leitrim, lost to Offaly) and won promotion to Division 2. Five championship games.
_meta.verification: All scorelines, venues and dates verified against primary tables and reports (RTE, gaa.ie, HoganStand, Irish News, The42). Full Wexford scorer list held for the Offaly quarter-final; partial for the Leitrim Round 3 win.

manager.name: John Hegarty
manager.note: Fourth season as Wexford manager. Oversaw promotion to Division 2 and a Tailteann Cup quarter-final run, despite a number of retirements (Kevin O'Grady, Mick Furlong and Conor Carthy) thinning the panel.

source.snippet: John Hegarty is preparing for a fourth season as Wexford boss.

league.note: Won promotion from Division 3 to Division 2, a clear marker of progress in Hegarty's tenure.

source.snippet: The Model county, who will replace their opponents in Division Two next year after winning promotion.
Tailteann Cup quarter-finals Offaly 1-22 Wexford 1-17, Glenisk O'Connor Park - FT
Quarter-Final Louth 1-25 0-11 Wexford Netwatch Dr Cullen Park

results.note: Wexford had a bye to the Leinster quarter-final and were heavily beaten there (17 points) by eventual All-Ireland semi-finalists Louth, at a neutral venue (Louth playing home games away from the redeveloping Drogheda Park). Sent Wexford into the Tailteann Cup. Full scorer list not captured. Louth's perspective will live in Louth's file (Louth still alive, semi-finalist).

source.snippet: First Round 9 May Wexford 2-19 1-11 Limerick Chadwicks Wexford Park

results.note: An 11-point home win over Limerick (2025 Tailteann finalists) to open the campaign. Full scorer list not captured. Limerick's perspective is held in Limerick's file.

results.note: A seven-point Round 2A loss away to Fermanagh, dropping Wexford into the Round 3 knockout. Full scorer list not captured. Fermanagh's perspective is held in Fermanagh's file.

source.snippet: Up in Carrick-on-Shannon, Wexford did all their business in the first half by stretching 0-14 to no score clear of Leitrim... 0-20 to 1-12.

results.note: A five-point away win, all but settled in a remarkable first half in which Wexford led 0-14 to 0-0. Both sides finished with 14 men (Conor Quinn and Leitrim's Liam Coleman sent off); a late Jack Kelly goal trimmed the margin. Partial scorer list (Nolan and goalkeeper Darragh Brooks both kicking two-pointers itemised). Leitrim's perspective is held in Leitrim's file.

scorers.note: 1 two-pointer; a fortuitous 24th-minute goal (a two-point attempt that dropped into the net)

source.snippet: Wexford: Darragh Brooks (0-2, 2pf)... Dylan Furlong (0-2, 2p)... Eoghan Nolan (1-3, 1 x 2p), Glen Malone (0-2)... Cathal Kehoe (0-2, 1 x 2p)... Mark Rossiter (0-4, 1 x 2p).

results.note: Elimination game. Wexford led 1-13 to 0-10 at half-time with the wind, their first four scores all two-pointers, but were outscored 1-12 to 0-4 in the second half as Offaly surged through, Jack Bryant's goal the turning point. A notable five two-pointers for Wexford (including two from goalkeeper Darragh Brooks). Full scorer list. Offaly's perspective is held in Offaly's file.

fields_not_found: Full Wexford scorer lists for the Louth Leinster QF, the Limerick R1 win and the Fermanagh R2A loss (scorelines verified)
collection_meta.notes: Wexford is a light-to-mid Tailteann file with a clear marker of progress (promotion to Division 2) and a quarter-final run that ended at Offaly's hands. The interesting editorial detail is the two-point shooting: Wexford hit five two-pointers in the Offaly quarter-final alone, two of them from goalkeeper Darragh Brooks, a genuine two-point free specialist, with Eoghan Nolan and Mark Rossiter the other range threats, this is a candidate data point for any Pulse Intelligence piece on the two-point arc and on goalkeeper scoring. John Hegarty's fourth season. A verification pass should fill the Louth, Limerick and Fermanagh scorer lists.
